text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Unit tests check individual components, like a single function or method, to ensure they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests go further, verifying that different parts of the system interact smoothly together. A popular approach is test-driven development (TDD), where you write a test before writing the actual code. This process forces you to clarify the expected behaviour upfront. Ultimately, thorough testing builds confidence. When you update code, a solid set of tests quickly catches new bugs, letting you make changes more safely and efficiently.
